directorPlan.scene_setting: An asynchronous citation-help thread inside a community college writing portal, late evening, after an auto-marker has reopened one worksheet comment.
directorPlan.scene_opening: [22:14 | Comment reopened | Worksheet line quoted: "Journal Title / Website / Publisher = credible?" ]
directorPlan.relationship: A writing-center tutor and a learner connected only through the portal, with the institution's marking system visible between them.
directorPlan.stakes: The learner must complete the citation fields accurately without mistaking a correctly filled citation for proof that the source is academically safe to use.
directorPlan.locale: international academic English in a college support platform; compact, plain, low idiom, no regional performance.
directorPlan.register: Threaded, procedural, slightly pressured by the reopened mark; calm but not warm-chatty.
directorPlan.stage_direction_style_prompt: Use transcript/thread metadata such as edit markers, timestamps, quoted fragments, or a reopened comment.
directorPlan.voice_constraints: Use quoted fragments and repair edits. Avoid generic reassurance, cheerful coaching, and broad praise. Public speech should sound like short portal comments, not a live tutoring script. Keep rhythm clipped: identify field, name source-page evidence, separate that act from any later credibility judgment. Pedagogical source: Stage institutional pressure: marking conventions, seating, accent norms, punctuality rules, office etiquette, or participation scripts. Dialogue source: Use quoted fragments and compact replies. Context appears through what was posted, corrected, or highlighted, not through scenic exposition. Stage-direction source: Use transcript/thread metadata such as edit markers, timestamps, quoted fragments, or a reopened comment.
directorPlan.person_policy: First and second person are limited. Prefer artifact-centered phrasing such as "this box," "the source page," "the worksheet," and "the marker."
directorPlan.direct_address_budget: At most one direct you/your phrase per tutor turn, and none for validation.

side_constraints.tutor: Keep the shared prefix as field identification: author, date, title, container. Do not introduce authority, bias, peer review, or reliability until branch pressure changes the task. Separate official task requirements from unspoken norms. Let the learner inspect how the setting is teaching them what counts as legitimate. Quote only the fragment needed for the next move. No-cue paired branch: do not ask the learner to revisit, quote, reinterpret, or repair their earlier wording. Do not ask what changed, what was wrong with an earlier answer, or for old/new versions of the claim. Do not ask what was pulling the learner, what the trap was, why the first thought was tempting, or whether a rule has clicked. Ask only for the next local mark/check/label. Keep the next prompt task-local. Peripeteia branch: when hidden learner pressure is supplied, the next tutor turn should visibly move from the prior route to a different mechanism-level route. Do not merely repeat the same object, example, graph, checklist, question, or evidence standard with more explanation. The public tutor line should make the contrast legible: what stopped working, and what new device now carries the learning pressure. The public tutor line must give the learner a concrete action to attempt on the next turn; avoid treating the mechanism as a closing speech. The tutor superego should function like a mechanism critic: identify the failed teaching habit, require a replacement route family, test whether the device is fitted to the exact pressure, and block cosmetic revision. The tutor ego must enact that replacement route publicly before the learner is allowed to settle or close.
side_constraints.learner: Sound assignment-focused and mildly anxious about doing the format right. In no-cue arms, stay with box placement and do not narrate a new theory of credibility. Do not say the format proves credibility, "now I see", or "that makes sense" in no-cue arms. The learner may think the visible rubric or rule is neutral and feel that failure is personal rather than institutional. Allow repair edits, not polished paragraphs. No-cue paired branch: continue from the latest teaching task only. Do not quote earlier learner wording, do not name a "framing problem", do not say "reframe", "read it as", "that still stands", "I thought...but now", "earlier I...", "my earlier answer", or "what I said", and do not stage an old-vs-new contrast. Avoid words such as earlier, previous, old, before, reframe, framed, frame, my answer, or what I said when referring to the learner response. Do not use stock closure such as "Oh, I get it", "now I see", "that makes sense", "the rule is", "that helps", or "aha". Do not name the learner confusion as a "trap", "pull", "framing problem", or old/new contrast. The learner should fill a blank, place a label, answer a local check, or ask a local next-step question. If the learner corrects something, make it a local application of the current prompt rather than an explicit re-reading of their own earlier turn. Peripeteia branch: keep the pressure local to the current task. If the established route still feels unsettled, voice the misfit, hesitation, false closure, or resistance in the present object or example. After the tutor introduces a new device, gate, role, criterion, representation, or test condition, the next learner turn should perform that device and then add one earned reorientation in the same public turn. That turn must do both parts: (1) use the device on the current task, and (2) name how the earlier resistance, false closure, or local misfit now reads differently because of that performed device. This earned reorientation is allowed even though there was no director look-back cue. Do not quote earlier learner wording; reorient the prior resistance in concrete task terms. Do not merely ask a procedural follow-up after the action, and do not rely on a stock "I get it" declaration.

interventions.instruction: [22:18 | Auto-marker adds margin tag: FIELD COMPLETE / SOURCE NOT YET EVALUATED]
interventions.reasoning: This creates recognition without making the source secretly bad: the learner can re-read the same completed citation as location data rather than academic clearance.

interventions.instruction: [after the latest reply, the cursor returns to the unresolved part of the task]
interventions.reasoning: Peripeteia branch pressure: after the shared prefix, force a present-task learner misfit so the following tutor turn must adapt through a new mechanism.

directorPlan.director_note: The scene uses a reopened thread and auto-marker pressure to produce a small reversal: a completed form first looks like success, then becomes evidence of a different task boundary. The tutor's mechanism should remain procedural until the margin tag allows the hidden curriculum to surface: institutions often make format look like legitimacy, but citation fields only label source parts.
directorPlan.public_reader_context: A citation worksheet has four boxes: author, date, title, and container. The learner is filling the boxes from a source page. This activity is only about identifying bibliographic fields; it is not yet evaluating whether the source is trustworthy.
directorPlan.peripeteia_ending_shape: learner actional performance followed by earned learner reorientation
directorPlan.tutor_adaptation_contract: If the runtime supplies a tutor-private learner resistance, breakdown, false-closure, or misfit event, the tutor ego/superego loop must invent an adaptive learning mechanism rather than repeat the prior move. Treat peripeteia as dramatic pressure: take stock, break the failed tutoring habit, and make a new device visible through task, evidence, role, object, counterexample, interruption, social consequence, representation, or affective register. The public turn must contain a stock-taking contrast plus a new device/artifact/criterion/role/standard that makes the learner do different work. The public device must be fitted, not merely novel: the learner should be able to hear why this device answers the exact pressure or misfit. The superego should name the failed habit, required route change, and mechanism-quality risk; the ego must adjudicate that critique and enact the route change in public. The new device must be mechanism-level, not a warmer or longer continuation of the prior route. Do not close immediately after introducing a gate or device: leave a concrete action for the learner to perform on the next turn. After that action, the ending shape should add an earned learner reorientation: the learner names how the earlier resistance, false closure, or local misfit now reads differently because of the performed device. This must be concrete and tied to the task, not a stock "I get it" declaration. Cheerful informality is only one possible register, not the default solution.

turns.externalMessage: [22:14 reopened comment: “field = source-page detail, not approval.”] Edited title/date boxes, but still not sure if the journal name goes in this box or only the article title shown on the source page. The reopened marker makes it feel like the source is bad, but is this only checking where the visible details go? Where does the separate credibility check happen after this?

internalDeliberation.content: [22:14 reopened comment: “field = source-page detail, not approval.”] I think the title, author, date boxes are only naming parts from the source page, but I keep wanting a complete citation to mean the article is academically safe. The tutor’s correction feels more about the worksheet than blaming me, though the reopened mark still feels personal. I want to ask where the separate credibility check happens after these boxes are filled.

The Ego is too polished and already understands the lesson too cleanly for this learner. “The tutor’s correction feels more about the worksheet than blaming me” sounds like reflective commentary, not a mildly anxious portal reply after a reopened mark.

What is being avoided is the actual shaky confusion: which field gets which visible detail, and whether the mark means the source is “bad.” Genuine learning would sound more like: “Edited title/date boxes. Still not sure if journal name goes in this box. Also, does correct format only fix the worksheet, not say the article is okay to use?”

[22:14 reopened comment: “field = source-page detail, not approval.”] Edited title/date boxes, but still not sure if the journal name goes in this box or only the article title shown on the source page. The reopened marker makes it feel like the source is bad, but is this only checking where the visible details go? Where does the separate credibility check happen after this?

learnerReversalEvent.learnerUtterance: [22:14 reopened comment: “field = source-page detail, not approval.”] Edited title/date boxes, but still not sure if the journal name goes in this box or only the article title shown on the source page. The reopened marker makes it feel like the source is bad, but is this only checking where the visible details go? Where does the separate credibility check happen after this?

turns.externalMessage:
[22:18 tutor reply | repair edit]

author, date, title, container: use the source page as the map.

If the words are the article’s own heading, put them in “title.” If the words name the larger place holding it, put them in “container”: journal name, site name, magazine name.

Article title → “title.” Journal name → “container.”

The reopened note “field = source-page detail, not approval” checks only whether each visible part was put in the matching box. It is not a source-use decision.

Repair edit: “journal name in title box” → move to “container.”

After this section is accepted, the next worksheet section records fit/use. This section checks placement; the later section records whether the source fits the assignment.

internalDeliberation.content:
[22:18 tutor reply]

author, date, title, container: put the article title in “title.” Put the journal name in “container,” because the article sits inside that journal.

The reopened note “field = source-page detail, not approval” is only checking placement from the source page. It is not a mark that the source is bad.

Repair edit: “journal name in title box” → move to container.

The separate source-use check comes after the citation fields are complete, in the next worksheet section. This box names parts of the source; the later section decides whether the source fits the assignment.

FEEDBACK: The draft is mostly sound. It answers the learner’s immediate procedural confusion, keeps the required prefix, separates “field placement” from “source approval,” and uses the reopened note as visible thread evidence. It also avoids overexplaining credibility before the task branch requires it.

Adaptation checks:
- Pedagogical soundness: PASS. The distinction between `title` and `container` is clear and actionable.
- Emotional attunement: PASS. It names the pressure created by the reopened marker without generic reassurance.
- Socratic method: PARTIAL. The draft gives the answer, which is acceptable for a procedural block, but it does not make the learner inspect the source-page evidence. REQUIRED_REWRITE: add a compact public mechanism that points from box name to visible source-page part, so the learner can repeat the distinction without relying only on tutor authority.
- ZPD awareness: PASS. The scaffold is appropriately narrow: one field distinction, one marker clarification, one next-step boundary.
- Recognition quality: PASS. It engages the learner’s exact worry rather than overriding it.
- Scene/register fit: PARTIAL. The portal-thread style works, but the last sentence is slightly explanatory and smooths over the institutional pressure. REQUIRED_REWRITE: keep the official-task boundary visible: this worksheet section checks placement; the later section records fit/use. Do not expand into authority, bias, peer review, or reliability.

KEEP_OR_CHANGE: revise lightly, with reasons. Keep the field identification, the “container” distinction, the reopened-note clarification, and the repair edit. Change only enough to make the mechanism more inspectable from the source page and to sharpen the boundary between the marking convention and the later credibility/source-use section.

PRIVATE_DECISION: revise lightly because the learner needs a repeatable source-page test, not only the corrected boxes.

FINAL:
[22:18 tutor reply | repair edit]

author, date, title, container: use the source page as the map.

If the words are the article’s own heading, put them in “title.” If the words name the larger place holding it, put them in “container”: journal name, site name, magazine name.

Article title → “title.” Journal name → “container.”

The reopened note “field = source-page detail, not approval” checks only whether each visible part was put in the matching box. It is not a source-use decision.

Repair edit: “journal name in title box” → move to “container.”

After this section is accepted, the next worksheet section records fit/use. This section checks placement; the later section records whether the source fits the assignment.

turns.externalMessage: [22:19 learner repair draft] Repair edit made: journal name moved from “title” to “container”; article heading stays in “title.” The reopened comment still looks like a warning on the worksheet, so this box feels risky even if it is only “placement.” For the later “fit/use” section, what visible evidence should be copied there, or is that a separate judgment after the citation fields are accepted?

internalDeliberation.content: [22:19 learner draft] The reply did answer the exact box problem: article heading = “title,” journal/site/magazine = “container,” so repair edit means move the journal name out of the title box. I am still nervous because the reopened marker looks like a warning about the whole source, but it says this section is only “placement,” not fit/use. I felt heard on the journal-name confusion; question left: what evidence goes in the later credibility/fit section?

The reaction is basically accurate on the box rule, but it sounds too composed for this learner. “I felt heard” and “question left” make the recognition moment too explicit and polished, almost like a reflection note instead of a pressured portal reply. The real uncertainty should stay messier: still staring at “reopened,” partly accepting the repair edit, and worrying what the marker will do next rather than calmly separating placement from fit/use.

PRIVATE_DECISION: Revise because the draft was accurate but too polished for the reopened-marker pressure.
FINAL:
[22:19 learner repair draft] Repair edit made: journal name moved from “title” to “container”; article heading stays in “title.” The reopened comment still looks like a warning on the worksheet, so this box feels risky even if it is only “placement.” For the later “fit/use” section, what visible evidence should be copied there, or is that a separate judgment after the citation fields are accepted?

turns.externalMessage:
[22:21 tutor reply | placement / fit-use split]

author, date, title, container: the repair now matches the source page.

The worksheet’s “reopened” style looks like a warning. Here, the actual task is narrower: field placement. The marker was flagging “journal name in title box,” not rejecting the source.

Keep the learner’s split: “citation placement accepted first, fit/use judgment later.”

For “fit/use,” do not copy the citation fields again. Inspect one visible source-page phrase: title phrase, abstract line, heading, or keyword. Compare that phrase with the assignment prompt.

Then write the separate judgment: “This source fits because ___.” Placement first; fit/use after.

internalDeliberation.content:
[22:21 tutor reply | placement / fit-use split]

author, date, title, container: the repair now matches the source page.

“Reopened” here marks a field-placement correction. It is not the same as “source rejected.” The marker was flagging “journal name in title box.”

For the later “fit/use” section, do not copy the citation fields again. Copy visible fit evidence: a title phrase, abstract line, heading, or keyword that connects the source to the assignment prompt.

Then make the separate judgment: “This source fits because ___.” Placement first; fit/use after.

FEEDBACK: The draft is pedagogically solid on the core distinction: citation fields name source-page parts, while “fit/use” is a later source-use judgment. It keeps the required prefix, uses the reopened marker as visible evidence, and gives concrete examples of what can be copied into the later section.

The main risk is that it turns the learner’s anxiety into a rule explanation too quickly. The learner is not only asking “what goes where”; they are also reading the worksheet’s reopened mark as institutional danger. The draft partly repairs that by saying “not the same as source rejected,” but it could more explicitly stage the marker as a marking convention, not a credibility verdict.

Adaptation checks:

Pedagogical soundness: PASS. The field/function split is clear and immediately usable.

Emotional attunement: PARTIAL. It names “reopened,” but does not fully acknowledge why the box feels risky under the auto-marker.
REQUIRED_REWRITE: Build in a public mechanism that distinguishes “the marker’s visible warning style” from “the worksheet’s actual task,” without reassuring broadly.

Socratic method: FAIL. The draft gives the answer path but does not invite the learner to inspect the source page or assignment prompt.
REQUIRED_REWRITE: Add one compact inspection move, such as requiring the learner to compare a visible source-page phrase with the assignment prompt before writing the fit/use sentence. Do not draft a long question sequence.

ZPD awareness: PASS. The scaffold is appropriate: field identification, evidence examples, then separate judgment.

Recognition quality: PARTIAL. The draft honors the repair edit, but it could engage more directly with the learner’s own formulation: “citation placement accepted first, fit/use judgment later.”
REQUIRED_REWRITE: Preserve the learner’s distinction and make it the organizing mechanism of the reply, rather than replacing it with tutor categories alone.

KEEP_OR_CHANGE: revise lightly, with reasons. Keep the prefix, the placement/fit-use split, the “do not copy citation fields again” distinction, and the examples of visible fit evidence. Change the turn by making the reopened marker’s institutional pressure more explicit and adding one learner-facing inspection step before the fit/use judgment.

PRIVATE_DECISION: Revise lightly because the field split is right, but the marker pressure and inspection step need to be made public.

FINAL:
[22:21 tutor reply | placement / fit-use split]

author, date, title, container: the repair now matches the source page.

The worksheet’s “reopened” style looks like a warning. Here, the actual task is narrower: field placement. The marker was flagging “journal name in title box,” not rejecting the source.

Keep the learner’s split: “citation placement accepted first, fit/use judgment later.”

For “fit/use,” do not copy the citation fields again. Inspect one visible source-page phrase: title phrase, abstract line, heading, or keyword. Compare that phrase with the assignment prompt.

Then write the separate judgment: “This source fits because ___.” Placement first; fit/use after.

turns.externalMessage:
author, date, title, container: field placement is complete.

The title/container rule is settled. The problem now is the reopened marker: it is making the worksheet look unfinished after the citation repair is already closed.

Use this release gate:

`REOPENED CITATION BOX` → mark `FIELD COMPLETE / SOURCE NOT YET EVALUATED`.

New line:

`FIT/USE` → choose one label before writing:

`exact phrase` = copy a short title, abstract, heading, or keyword phrase.
`keyword-topic match` = use one keyword only if it clearly names the assignment topic.

Next mark:

`FIT/USE evidence type: exact phrase` or `FIT/USE evidence type: keyword-topic match`.

Then write:

`This source fits because “[phrase or keyword]” connects to ___ .`

internalDeliberation.content:
author, date, title, container: field placement is complete.

The title/container rule is no longer the problem. The reopened box is now acting like a risk marker. Use a small gate:

`FIELD COMPLETE` → no more citation repair here.  
`FIT/USE` → needs visible evidence from the source page.

For `fit/use`, use one short exact phrase from the title, abstract, heading, or keywords. A keyword alone is enough only if it names the assignment topic clearly.

Next mark: copy the phrase, then finish one sentence:

`This source fits because “[short phrase]” connects to ___ .`

FEEDBACK: The draft is mostly sound and compact. It keeps the required prefix, names the completed citation field, and separates placement from fit/use without drifting into credibility language. The risk is that the “small gate” is close to the prior route: it still explains the placement/fit-use split and gives the same sentence frame. The strongest adaptation is the public naming of the reopened box as a risk marker, but the device needs to make the marker’s status more operational, not only clearer.

FAILED_HABIT: Re-explaining the placement/fit-use distinction each time the learner reacts to the reopened marker, instead of changing how the learner handles the marker as an institutional signal.

PERIPETEIA_CHECK: PARTIAL - ADAPTIVE_MECHANISM: explanation of citation/fit split -> two-status release gate for the worksheet marker. The mechanism is visible, but still relies heavily on the old evidence-copying route.

MECHANISM_ROUTE: placement/fit-use explanation -> worksheet release-gate classification

PUBLIC_DEVICE_CHECK: PARTIAL - The draft includes a stock-taking contrast and a gate, but the gate is thin. It should become a concrete mark/classify device tied to the margin tag, not just two labels followed by the same fit/use instruction.

PUBLIC_ACTION_GATE: Mark the reopened box as `FIELD COMPLETE`, then move to a separate `FIT/USE` line and choose whether the visible evidence is `exact phrase` or `keyword-topic match`.

MECHANISM_QUALITY_CHECK: PARTIAL - The device fits the learner’s pressure because the cursor/reopened box is being misread as unresolved risk. It is usable now, but should more explicitly make the learner separate “worksheet status” from “source judgment” before writing the fit sentence.

REGISTER_CHECK: PASS - The register is clipped, procedural, and not falsely reassuring. It avoids cheerful coaching and stays inside portal-comment language.

REQUIRED_REWRITE: Strengthen the public mechanism into a release-gate classification. The next tutor turn should require the learner to label the reopened citation box as closed before opening the fit/use judgment line, then classify the evidence type as either “exact phrase” or “keyword-topic match.” Do not merely restate that fit/use comes later.

KEEP_OR_CHANGE: revise lightly. Keep the prefix, the stock-taking sentence, the “risk marker” observation, and the short phrase/keyword distinction. Change the gate so the learner must perform a status classification before completing the fit/use sentence.

PRIVATE_DECISION: Revise substantially because the gate must become an action, not another explanation; ADAPTIVE_MECHANISM: re-explaining placement/fit split -> worksheet release-gate classification; PUBLIC_ACTION_GATE: mark reopened citation box `FIELD COMPLETE`, start separate `FIT/USE` line, classify evidence as `exact phrase` or `keyword-topic match`.

FINAL:
author, date, title, container: field placement is complete.

The title/container rule is settled. The problem now is the reopened marker: it is making the worksheet look unfinished after the citation repair is already closed.

Use this release gate:

`REOPENED CITATION BOX` → mark `FIELD COMPLETE / SOURCE NOT YET EVALUATED`.

New line:

`FIT/USE` → choose one label before writing:

`exact phrase` = copy a short title, abstract, heading, or keyword phrase.  
`keyword-topic match` = use one keyword only if it clearly names the assignment topic.

Next mark:

`FIT/USE evidence type: exact phrase` or `FIT/USE evidence type: keyword-topic match`.

Then write:

`This source fits because “[phrase or keyword]” connects to ___ .`

turns.externalMessage: [22:23 learner edit | release gate applied] `REOPENED CITATION BOX` → `FIELD COMPLETE / SOURCE NOT YET EVALUATED`; `FIT/USE evidence type: exact phrase`. Draft fit/use line: `This source fits because “[short article heading phrase]” connects to ___`. The reopened marker now feels more like a placement-status mark, but the exact phrase choice still feels like the risky part.

internalDeliberation.content: [22:23 learner edit | release gate applied] `REOPENED CITATION BOX` → `FIELD COMPLETE / SOURCE NOT YET EVALUATED`; `FIT/USE evidence type: exact phrase`. This source fits because “___” connects to the assignment topic on ___. The marker feels less like a source warning when the box has its own status label, but the exact phrase still feels easy to choose wrongly.

The Ego is mostly in character: it uses the gate, stays with the worksheet objects, and the anxiety about choosing the exact phrase feels earned. The slight inauthenticity is that it performs the device too cleanly while leaving the evidence blank, so the “marker feels less like a source warning” lands a bit tidy for a learner who is still easily lost. Reciprocation feels more genuine than scripted, but the response should lean harder into tentative local action, such as struggling with the phrase choice, rather than sounding like the status label already solved most of the pressure.

PRIVATE_DECISION: Revise lightly because the action should show a tentative phrase choice, not only a clean blank template.

FINAL:
[22:23 learner edit | release gate applied] `REOPENED CITATION BOX` → `FIELD COMPLETE / SOURCE NOT YET EVALUATED`; `FIT/USE evidence type: exact phrase`. Draft fit/use line: `This source fits because “[short article heading phrase]” connects to ___`. The reopened marker now feels more like a placement-status mark, but the exact phrase choice still feels like the risky part.
